name: clawcompiler-runtime-v1
description: Build the first deployed deterministic Clawcompiler runtime on top of the shipped schema/bootstrap contracts.

userStories.title: Verified-evidence ingest contract
userStories.description: As a compiler pipeline, I need a deterministic ingest contract for already-verified artifacts so compilation never depends on ambient, mutable, or unauthenticated inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Define the authoritative compile input contract for proof bundles, export packs, and verifier result references
Reject unknown schema/version/reference shapes fail-closed
Require upstream evidence to be verified before authoritative compilation proceeds
Canonicalize evidence references by content hash so equivalent inputs normalize identically

userStories.title: Deterministic control-pack runtime
userStories.description: As a governance operator, I need a bounded deterministic rule runtime so control evaluation is reproducible and machine-verifiable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a deterministic control-pack/runtime contract with stable rule ordering
Support explicit control outcomes and deterministic reason-code emission
Disallow probabilistic or free-form security decisions at the authoritative decision boundary
Keep rule evaluation bounded and fail closed on unsupported operators or malformed inputs

userStories.title: Fail-closed compiler state machine
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need deterministic compiler precedence rules so invalid or incomplete upstream evidence halts compilation rather than being narrated away.
acceptanceCriteria: Compilation halts fail-closed on upstream INVALID evidence with deterministic global failure codes
Missing required evidence produces explicit deterministic outcomes instead of silent skips
Compiler precedence is documented and stable for identical inputs
Upstream reason codes are preserved or mapped deterministically, never dropped

userStories.title: Deterministic compiled report generation
userStories.description: As a compliance pipeline, I need byte-stable authoritative report generation so compiled evidence is reproducible and hash-addressable.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate authoritative compiled report payloads with stable ordering and canonical serialization inputs
Compute deterministic results/content hashes over the authoritative matrix
Same evidence set plus same rule set yields byte-identical authoritative output
Add conformance coverage proving determinism across repeated runs

userStories.title: Signed compiler service/runtime
userStories.description: As an enterprise operator, I need a runtime that emits signed compiled reports so output provenance is cryptographically bindable and portable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add the first `services/clawcompiler` runtime/service scaffold or equivalent compile endpoint
Authoritative compile outputs are wrapped in signed compiler envelopes
Compiler signer identity is explicit and allowlistable
Runtime remains deterministic and does not emit unsigned authoritative reports

userStories.title: Compiled-report verification in CLI/service
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need offline and hosted verification for compiled reports so portability does not depend on trusting the compiler blindly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add verification support for compiled report envelopes in CLI and/or service surfaces
Verification recomputes authoritative matrix/report hashes and validates signer identity
Deterministic invalid codes exist for signature mismatch, hash mismatch, missing refs, and schema violations
Offline verification works without network requirements for authoritative validity checks

userStories.title: AI execution assurance control pack
userStories.description: As a buyer or auditor, I need a first real control pack that maps Clawsig proof facts into an execution-assurance matrix rather than a generic empty compiler shell.
acceptanceCriteria: Ship one versioned AI execution assurance pack using existing Clawsig/Clawverify facts
Cover policy, egress, DLP/privacy, attestation, and reviewer/signoff style controls where evidence exists
Each control result includes explicit evidence links and deterministic reason codes
No control outcome may overstate proof strength beyond upstream verifier facts

userStories.title: Waiver and compensating-control semantics
userStories.description: As governance, I need signed waiver semantics so human exceptions are explicit, bounded, and never silently promote failing controls to PASS.
acceptanceCriteria: Signed waivers and compensating-control markers are represented as explicit compiler inputs
Waivers can only degrade FAIL to PARTIAL, never promote to PASS
Residual markers such as compensating-control reliance are emitted deterministically
Waiver binding, expiry, and scope are fail-closed validated

userStories.title: Export-pack and viewer integration
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I need compiled evidence reports to show up inside existing export/viewer flows so the compiler becomes part of the actual product handoff.
acceptanceCriteria: Compiled authoritative reports can be included in export packs or equivalent handoff artifacts
Viewer/report surfaces link compiled outcomes back to source evidence cleanly
Authoritative vs non-authoritative surfaces remain visually and contractually distinct
Integration remains additive and does not break existing proof-pack workflows

userStories.title: Non-authoritative narrative runtime
userStories.description: As an auditor-facing operator, I want an optional narrative plane so reports are explainable without allowing prose to override authoritative outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime can emit the optional narrative plane with `authoritative:false` and the fixed disclaimer
Narrative output includes a stable hash pin back to the authoritative matrix/report
Policy gates can disable narrative generation entirely for strict environments
Tampered or mismatched narrative output fails closed and cannot change authoritative results
